prompt:
A length of 2 mm copper solder wick is pulled out from a small, round spool resting on an electronics workbench, the flat braided pattern of the wick clearly visible where it lies across a PCB pad. The end of the braid is slightly darkened and spread out, showing it has just absorbed solder from a joint. The spool itself is plain with an icon-only label and no readable text, emphasizing that it is a no-clean desoldering braid.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics station with soft, even lighting, a soldering iron stand and brass tip cleaner nearby, and organized component drawers fading into the background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or legible writing on the spool or tools, and a composition that centers the braid, its used tip, and the PCB pads while the surrounding workspace remains softly blurred.
